comparator: llama.cpp acd79d603 (Windows CPU-only build) /completion, greedy (temp 0/top_k 1/seed 0), -ngl 0 -ctk f32 -ctv f32 -fa off --no-repack -c 8704
camelid_backend: q8_0 runtime, RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB: GPU-resident decode for positions < 2090 (the VRAM-sized KV cap on this card, reported by build_resident_cuda_engine: weights 4315 MiB + 512 MiB headroom -> fits 2090 pos), CPU-fallback decode for positions >= 2090. Both buckets exceed 2090, so their tails are CPU-fallback decoded.
execution_note: Single-model runs only: the llama.cpp oracle was captured alone and its server killed before camelid was started (engines never co-resident), to stay within host RAM.
claim_boundary: Bounded 4096/8192-context (4088/8253 Qwen3 tokens) raw-decode greedy parity for the exact Qwen3-4B-Q8_0 row ONLY: camelid is token-AND-text-identical to the pinned llama.cpp acd79d603 oracle at 50 generated tokens on both buckets. On this 6 GiB card the decode path is GPU-resident for positions < 2090 and camelid CPU-fallback for positions >= 2090 (both buckets exceed the cap) — a bigger card would run these fully resident; the token stream is identical either way. NOT claimed: GPU-resident decode of these buckets on this card, model-native/larger context beyond 8192, other Qwen3 sizes/quants, or throughput. Complements the 512/1024/2048 bundle (fully GPU-resident) at qa/evidence-bundles/qwen3-4b-q8-context-512-2048-20260706T233000Z-head-eabe9dac74fa.
